Tests the length property of the arguments object in a function.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that tests the behavior of the `arguments` object in JavaScript. The function [`f_arg`](<#04-001f_arg>) returns the `arguments` object, which is an array-like object accessible inside functions that contains the values of the arguments passed to that function. The script includes an assertion that checks if the length of the `arguments` object returned by [`f_arg`](<#04-001f_arg>) is zero, which confirms that [`f_arg`](<#04-001f_arg>) is called without any arguments. This script provides narrow functionality focused on verifying the default behavior of the `arguments` object when no arguments are passed to a function.

### f\_arg<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/11/11.02/11.02.04/11.02.04-001.f_arg}} -->

- **Inputs**: None
- **Control Flow**:

- **Output**: The `arguments` object, which is an array-like object containing the arguments passed to the function.
